summary: Encryption and Audit Trail Test Case
description: Validate that patient data is encrypted using AES-256 and audit trails are stored for every database change.
objective: Verify that system complies with IEC 62304, ISO 13485, and FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for encryption and audit trail storage.
prerequisites: System must be configured to use AES-256 encryption
Database must be set up to store audit trails

test_steps.description: Enter a new patient record with sensitive data
Verify that the entered data is encrypted using AES-256
Perform an action that triggers a database change (e.g. update patient record)
Verify that the system stores an audit trail for the database change

expected_result: The system must encrypt patient data using AES-256 and store an audit trail for every database change.
test_data: Sample patient record with sensitive data (e.g. name, date of birth, medical history)
acceptance_criteria: System encrypts patient data using AES-256
System stores audit trails for every database change
